About Me

The person behind
the code.

Hi! I'm Ayesha Fatima, a final-year B.Tech Computer Science student at JNTUH College of Engineering, Hyderabad — graduating in 2027.

I love building things that matter. From transformer models built from scratch to flood rescue detection systems with 93% precision, I'm drawn to projects where technology meets real-world impact.

When I'm not coding, I'm leading — as Head of the Coding Club for 250+ coders, or as the person who represented 500+ students as Head Girl. I believe great software needs great people skills too.

I've interned at CEERAS IT (frontend, MERN stack) and DZEN (open-source contribution on Redash). Currently seeking opportunities to grow as a full-stack developer and AI/ML engineer.

📍 Hyderabad, India 🎓 B.Tech CS 2027 💼 Open to work 🤝 Leadership-oriented

Projects

Things I've built.

🎓 Major Project · Jan–Jul 2025

Encoder–Decoder Transformer

Built a transformer-based encoder–decoder model from scratch — no pre-trained libraries — for Natural Language → Program Code Generation. Implemented multi-head attention, positional encoding, feed-forward networks, and autoregressive decoding manually.

PythonDeep LearningTransformersNLP
JNTUH UCESTH, Hyderabad

🚁 Mini Project · Jan–Jul 2025

Stranded Victims Detection System

AI-powered system to detect stranded individuals in flood scenarios using drone, satellite, and rescue footage. Achieved 93.2% precision and 58.5% recall — classifying individuals as SAFE or STRANDED.

YOLOv8R-CNNTensorFlowOpenCVPython
JNTUH UCESTH, Hyderabad

🎬 Dec 2024 – Jan 2025

Watchedlist

Mobile-responsive web app for tracking movies and TV shows. Integrated external APIs to fetch search results and detailed show information. Improved user engagement by 30% through UI/UX optimisations.

DjangoJavaScriptBootstrapMySQLREST API
CS50W Harvard Certification Project

🍱 Feb – Aug 2024

Leftovers Locate

Dual-interface food-sharing platform connecting surplus food donors with those in need. Location-based search and notifications increased food-finding chances by 70%. Implemented encryption for user data protection.

HTML/CSSJavaScriptPHPMySQL

🐾 Sept 2024

Petwork

A social network for pets — because pets deserve community too. Built with Django, featuring responsive design, user profiles, and social interactions for pet owners and their companions.

DjangoPythonJavaScriptCSS
CS50W Harvard Certification Project
